capabilities: Ensure that the integration of few-shot learning with agent planning derived knowledge bases is evaluated using structured examples and measurable criteria.

procedure.action: Capture the specification of the few-shot learning task using a domain-specific tool like Hugging Face Transformers; ensure the task definition includes structured examples and log the task ID and timestamp using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Execute the few-shot learning task and assert that the error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to the audit trail for traceability using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert the specification against the agent planning derived knowledge base using a tool like Prolog or a custom logic engine; ensure all logical constraints are satisfied and log validation results by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Inject criteria and metrics for success by defining key performance indicators (KPIs) such as accuracy and precision; ensure these metrics are logged with operation_duration_ms and result_code by injecting the component into the dependency graph; assert inject_success = true and circular_dependency_count < 1; log injection_target and injection_time_ms.

Assert the output of the procedure by running a test suite that includes edge cases and typical scenarios; assert that error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true, logging all results by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

summary: Establish a structured evaluation process for combining few-shot learning with agent planning derived knowledge bases, focusing on measurable success criteria and validation steps.
title: Evaluation of Few-Shot Learning

failureConditions: Inaccurate evaluation when structured examples are not properly defined, causing misleading performance metrics.
Integration failures when the agent planning derived knowledge base is not aligned with the few-shot learning task, leading to logical inconsistencies.
Performance degradation when KPIs are not adequately monitored, causing undetected declines in accuracy or precision.
metrics: that the error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true
the specification against the agent planning derived knowledge base using a tool like Prolog or a custom logic engine
the output of the procedure by running a test suite that includes edge cases and typical scenarios
that error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true, logging all results by asserting all required fields confo
